Table of Contents
Item 1A. Risk Factors .......... 14

Item 1A. Risk Factors

Our business faces intense competition across all of our product lines, and competitive pressures could reduce our pricing power and erode operating margins over time. We depend on a concentrated base of suppliers for key components, and disruptions in that supply chain have in the past affected, and could in the future affect, our ability to manufacture and deliver products on schedule.

Global economic conditions, including inflation and fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates, may adversely affect demand for our products and the cost of our inputs. We are subject to extensive and evolving regulation in the jurisdictions where we operate, and changes in laws or enforcement priorities could increase our compliance costs.

During the most recent fiscal year, management identified a material weakness in our internal control over financial reporting relating to the review of complex revenue arrangements. We have not yet fully remediated this material weakness, and we cannot assure you that additional material weaknesses will not be identified in the future. Our independent registered public accounting firm has expressed substantial doubt about our ability to continue as a going concern. We are in active discussions with our lenders regarding covenant waivers, and there can be no assurance that we will obtain such waivers on acceptable terms or at all.

We are the subject of a newly filed regulatory enforcement action alleging violations of disclosure requirements, the outcome of which is uncertain and could result in material fines and restrictions on our operations. A significant customer representing a substantial portion of our revenue has notified us of its intent to materially reduce purchases.

Item 1B. Unresolved Staff Comments
None.
